Replication Code: Hospital Attacks Sudan

Lilli Blank, Franziska Gekeler

2/7/2026

Why do conflict parties attack hospitals in Sudan?

This markdown shows the code used for our thesis “Why do conflict parties attack health care infrastructure? Understanding attacks on hospitals in Sudan”. You’ll find more information on the data sources in Section 3, for possible replication. The code is structured as follows:

Table of contents

  1. Loading the necessary packages
  2. Load the datasets we use
  3. Create spatial unit of analysis: grids
  4. Prepare datasets
  5. Create spatial and temporal unit of analysis (grid week)
  6. Descriptive analysis
  7. Merge all data (ZINB preparation)
  8. ZINB analysis
  9. Statistical and robustness checks

Loading the necessary packages

library(broom)
library(dplyr)
library(fuzzyjoin)
library(ggplot2)
library(leaflet)
library(lubridate)
library(readxl)
library(reshape2)
library(scales)
library(tidyverse)
library(stargazer)
library(sf)
library(sp)
library(terra) 
library(knitr)
library(pscl)
library(performance)
library(lmtest)
library(MASS)

Load the datasets we use

ACLED_data <- read.csv("/Users/Lilli/Documents/R/MasterThesis/ACLED Data_2025-11-04.csv")
InsecurityInsight_data <- read_excel (("/Users/Lilli/Documents/R/MasterThesis/2016-2025-attacks-on-health-care-incident-data.xlsx"))
ConflictIntensity_data <- read.csv("/Users/Lilli/Documents/R/MasterThesis/ACLED Data_2025-11-04_ALL-ATTACKS.csv")
Geo_data1 <- st_read(("/Users/Lilli/Documents/R/MasterThesis/gadm41_SDN_0.shp")) 
## Reading layer `gadm41_SDN_0' from data source 
##   `/Users/Lilli/Documents/R/MasterThesis/gadm41_SDN_0.shp' using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
## Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 2 fields
## Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON
## Dimension:     XY
## Bounding box:  xmin: 21.83895 ymin: 8.679119 xmax: 38.8493 ymax: 23.14515
## CRS:           NA
pop_raster <- rast("/Users/Lilli/Documents/R/MasterThesis/sdn_pop_2023_CN_1km_R2025A_UA_v1.tif")

Create spatial unit of analysis: grids

#plot Sudan and create grids
#Plotting Sudan (as a whole, without grids)----
Sudan <- Geo_data1

print(Sudan)
## Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 2 fields
## Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON
## Dimension:     XY
## Bounding box:  xmin: 21.83895 ymin: 8.679119 xmax: 38.8493 ymax: 23.14515
## CRS:           NA
##   GID_0 COUNTRY                       geometry
## 1   SDN   Sudan MULTIPOLYGON (((24.22412 8....
plot(st_geometry(Sudan))

st_crs(Sudan)
## Coordinate Reference System: NA
Sudan <- st_set_crs(Sudan, 4326)
sudan_meters <- st_transform(Sudan, 3857)
st_bbox(sudan_meters)
##      xmin      ymin      xmax      ymax 
## 2431100.7  969871.3 4324684.7 2649581.2
#We're creating grids now---- 
grid_size <- 30000   # 30 km

grid <- st_make_grid(
  sudan_meters,
  cellsize = grid_size,
  square = TRUE
)

grid <- st_sf(
  grid_id = 1:length(grid),
  geometry = grid
)

grid_sudan <- st_intersection(grid, sudan_meters)

nrow(grid_sudan)
## [1] 2424
#create master-grid
grid_sf <- grid_sudan %>%
  mutate(grid_id = row_number())

#plotting of grids
spatial_unit_of_analysis <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = grid_sudan, fill = NA, color = "grey70", size = 0.15) +
  geom_sf(data = sudan_meters, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman") +
  labs(
    title = "30 × 30 km Grid Cells in Sudan",
    subtitle = "Spatial units of analysis",
    caption = "Source: Author's own grid based on GADM boundary"
  )

print(spatial_unit_of_analysis)

Prepare datasets

#Data Preparation -> descriptive Analysis

#Insecurity Insight /SHCC Data-Set ----
#Filter for Sudan only
sudan_HIA_data <- subset(InsecurityInsight_data, Country == "Sudan")
# put date into date format
sudan_HIA_data$event_date <- as.Date(sudan_HIA_data$Date, format = "%Y-%m-%d")
#filter events starting from 2023
sudan_HIA_data_2023 <- sudan_HIA_data %>%
  filter(event_date >= as.Date("2023-01-01"))


#filtering attacks on hospitals (health facilities) - Sudan_hospital_attacks_data is Data-Set we're working with
sudan_hospital_attacks_data <- sudan_HIA_data_2023 %>%
  filter(
    `Number of Attacks on Health Facilities Reporting Destruction` > 0 |
      `Number of Attacks on Health Facilities Reporting Damaged` > 0 |
      `Forceful Entry into Health Facility` > 0 |
      `Occupation of Health Facility` > 0
  )

#filter for events with Geo-precision
sudan_hospital_attacks_data <- sudan_hospital_attacks_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(!(`Geo Precision` == '(4) Province, State, Governorate'| `Geo Precision` == ' (6) Country' ))


#bring it in geometric form 
sudan_hospital_attacks_sf <- st_as_sf(
  sudan_hospital_attacks_data,
  coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"),
  crs = 4326
)

sudan_hospital_attacks_sf <- st_transform(
  sudan_hospital_attacks_sf,
  st_crs(sudan_meters)
)

attacks_coords <- sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    x = st_coordinates(.)[,1],
    y = st_coordinates(.)[,2]
  )

attacks_by_location <- attacks_coords %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  dplyr::count(x, y, name = "n_attacks")

attacks_locations_sf <- st_as_sf(
  attacks_by_location,
  coords = c("x", "y"),
  crs = st_crs(sudan_meters)
)

#prepare Insecurity Insight Data for grid week ---- 
  sudan_hospital_attacks_sf <- sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>%
  mutate(
    event_date = as.Date(event_date),
    week = floor_date(event_date, "week")
  )

#map attacks on grids
#first bring data to common metric coordinate
sudan_hospital_attacks_sf <- st_transform(
  sudan_hospital_attacks_sf,
  st_crs(grid_sudan)
)

hospital_attacks_grid <- st_join(
  sudan_hospital_attacks_sf,
  grid_sudan,
  left = FALSE
)

hospital_attacks_grid_week <- hospital_attacks_grid %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  count(grid_id, week, name = "hospital_attacks")

#Filter for RSF and SAF - attacks_by_actor new dataset 
attacks_by_actor <- sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  dplyr::count(`Reported Perpetrator Name`, name = "n_attacks") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n_attacks))

#see how many attacks per actor 
attacks_by_actor
## # A tibble: 6 × 2
##   `Reported Perpetrator Name`                 n_attacks
##   <chr>                                           <int>
## 1 Rapid Support Forces                              165
## 2 Sudanese Armed Forces                              41
## 3 No Information                                     20
## 4 Rapid Support Forces, Sudanese Armed Forces        15
## 5 Unidentified Armed Actor                           13
## 6 South Sudan People's Defence Force                  1
#see percentage of attacks per actor
attacks_by_actor <- attacks_by_actor %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    share = round(n_attacks / sum(n_attacks) * 100, 1)
  )
attacks_by_actor
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   `Reported Perpetrator Name`                 n_attacks share
##   <chr>                                           <int> <dbl>
## 1 Rapid Support Forces                              165  64.7
## 2 Sudanese Armed Forces                              41  16.1
## 3 No Information                                     20   7.8
## 4 Rapid Support Forces, Sudanese Armed Forces        15   5.9
## 5 Unidentified Armed Actor                           13   5.1
## 6 South Sudan People's Defence Force                  1   0.4
#ACLED territorial changes Data ----
territorial_change_data <- ACLED_data

#filter for events with geo and time precision lower than 3
territorial_change_data <- territorial_change_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(!(geo_precision == 3 | time_precision == 3))

#check, what kinds of sub_event are in the data
territorial_change_data %>% count(sub_event_type, sort =TRUE)
##                         sub_event_type    n
## 1         Looting/property destruction 1768
## 2             Change to group/activity  765
## 3                                Other  668
## 4         Government regains territory  314
## 5    Non-violent transfer of territory  231
## 6                Disrupted weapons use  220
## 7  Non-state actor overtakes territory  212
## 8                            Agreement  160
## 9                              Arrests  160
## 10    Headquarters or base established    5
#filter for the territorial changes
territorial_events <- territorial_change_data %>%
  filter(sub_event_type %in% c(
    "Government regains territory",
    "Non-state actor overtakes territory", 
    "Non-violent transfer of territory"
  ))

territorial_events_sf <- territorial_events %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326) %>%
  st_transform(st_crs(grid_sudan))

#ACLED Data: prepare data for conflict intensity ----
conflict_intensity_data <- ConflictIntensity_data

#check the disorder_type, and event_type
conflict_intensity_data %>% count(disorder_type, sort =TRUE)
##            disorder_type     n
## 1     Political violence 12043
## 2 Strategic developments  2814
## 3         Demonstrations   270
conflict_intensity_data %>% count(event_type, sort =TRUE)
##                   event_type    n
## 1                    Battles 4954
## 2 Violence against civilians 4046
## 3 Explosions/Remote violence 2980
## 4     Strategic developments 2814
## 5                      Riots  333
#filter for relevant events and mutate dates for further calculation
conflict_intensity_clean <- conflict_intensity_data %>%
  filter(disorder_type == "Political violence") %>%
  mutate(
    event_date = as.Date(event_date),
    week = floor_date(event_date, "week")
  ) %>%
  filter(event_date >= as.Date("2023-01-01"))

#sanity check
nrow(conflict_intensity_clean)
## [1] 9864
summary(conflict_intensity_clean$event_date)
##         Min.      1st Qu.       Median         Mean      3rd Qu.         Max. 
## "2023-01-01" "2023-08-20" "2024-01-04" "2024-01-12" "2024-06-09" "2024-11-04"
table(conflict_intensity_clean$event_type)
## 
##                    Battles Explosions/Remote violence 
##                       4271                       2938 
##                      Riots Violence against civilians 
##                         18                       2637
#lose the events where Geo-precision and time precision has value 3
conflict_intensity_clean <- conflict_intensity_clean %>%
  dplyr::filter(!(geo_precision == 3 | time_precision == 3))

#check if entries with value 3 are gone 
sum(conflict_intensity_clean$geo_precision == 3, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 0
sum(conflict_intensity_clean$time_precision == 3, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 0
#mutate in sf 
conflict_intensity_sf <- conflict_intensity_clean %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326) %>%
  st_transform(st_crs(grid_sudan))
#and map on grids
conflict_grid <- st_join(
  conflict_intensity_sf,
  grid_sudan,
  left = FALSE
)
#and aggregate conflict intensity per grid week 
conflict_intensity_grid_week <- conflict_grid %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  count(grid_id, week, name = "conflict_intensity")

summary(conflict_intensity_grid_week$conflict_intensity)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   1.000   1.000   1.000   3.698   3.000  93.000
#prepare WorldPop Data for control variable ----

pop_raster
## class       : SpatRaster 
## dimensions  : 1625, 2044, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr)
## resolution  : 0.008333333, 0.008333333  (x, y)
## extent      : 21.81667, 38.85, 8.683334, 22.225  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax)
## coord. ref. : lon/lat WGS 84 (EPSG:4326) 
## source      : sdn_pop_2023_CN_1km_R2025A_UA_v1.tif 
## name        : global_pop_2023_CN_1km_R2025A_v1
# 0) Check: grid_id darf NICHT neu nummeriert werden
stopifnot("grid_id" %in% names(grid_sudan))

# 2) Raster in CRS von grid_sudan projizieren 
pop_raster_proj <- project(pop_raster, st_crs(grid_sudan)$wkt)

# 3) grid_sudan -> SpatVector
grid_vect <- vect(grid_sudan)

# 4) Population pro Grid extrahieren (Summe)
population_grid <- extract(
  pop_raster_proj,
  grid_vect,
  fun = sum,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

#rename column
population_grid <- population_grid %>%
  rename(population_2023 = global_pop_2023_CN_1km_R2025A_v1)

names(population_grid)
## [1] "ID"              "population_2023"

Create spatial and temporal unit of analysis (grid week)

#Creation of grid-weeks 
#create vector of weeks 
weeks <- sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(week = floor_date(Date, "week")) %>%
  distinct(week)
#grid x week
panel_df <- expand.grid(
  grid_id = grid_sf$grid_id,
  week = weeks$week
)

#aggregate territorial events on grid-week

territorial_events_sf <- territorial_events_sf %>%
  mutate(
    event_date = as.Date(event_date),
    week = floor_date(event_date, "week")
  )

territorial_grid <- st_join(
  territorial_events_sf,
  grid_sudan,
  left = FALSE
)

territorial_counts <- territorial_grid %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  count(grid_id, week, name = "n_territorial_events")

#check (for sanity)
summary(territorial_counts$n_territorial_events)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   1.000   2.000   2.000   3.065   2.000  18.000
sum(is.na(territorial_counts$week))
## [1] 0

Descriptive analysis

#helper function ----
save_png <- function(plot, name, w = 18, h = 12,
                     path = "output") {
  dir.create(path, showWarnings = FALSE)
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(path, paste0(name, ".png")),
    plot = plot,
    width = w, height = h, units = "cm",
    dpi = 300,
    device = "png"
  )
}

#Plot: counting the attacks against hospitals ----
weekly_attacks <- sudan_hospital_attacks_data %>%
  mutate(week = floor_date(event_date, "week")) %>%
  group_by(week) %>%
  summarise(count = n())

# create plot
p1 <- ggplot(weekly_attacks, aes(x = week, y = count)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Attacks on hospitals in Sudan",
       x = "Week",
       y = "Number of attacks") +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman")
print(p1)

#check with conflict intensity
weekly_attacks_2 <- conflict_intensity_clean %>%
  mutate(week = floor_date(event_date, "week")) %>%
  group_by(week) %>%
  summarise(count = n())

scale_factor <- max(weekly_attacks$count, na.rm = TRUE) /
  max(weekly_attacks_2$count, na.rm = TRUE)

p2 <- ggplot() +
  geom_line(
    data = weekly_attacks,
    aes(x = week, y = count),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = weekly_attacks_2,
    aes(x = week, y = count * scale_factor),
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Number of hospital attacks",
    sec.axis = sec_axis(
      ~ . / scale_factor,
      name = "Conflict intensity"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Hospital attacks and conflict intensity in Sudan",
    x = "Week"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman")

print(p2)

#define baseline and escalation ans (incl. rubustness check) ----
#rename column count
weekly_attacks <- weekly_attacks%>%
rename(n_attacks = count )

threshold <- quantile(weekly_attacks$n_attacks, 0.80, na.rm = TRUE)

weekly_attacks <- weekly_attacks %>%
  mutate(
    escalation_80 = ifelse(n_attacks >= threshold, "Escalation", "Baseline")
  )
#what's our 80th percentile
p80 <- quantile(weekly_attacks$n_attacks, probs = 0.80, na.rm = TRUE)
p80
## 80% 
##   4
#check distribution
summary(weekly_attacks$n_attacks)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   1.000   1.000   2.000   2.931   3.000  24.000
#histogram 
hist(weekly_attacks$n_attacks, breaks = 20)

attack_threshold <- 4
#plot weekly series, threshold and the escalation marking
escalation_plot_80 <- ggplot(weekly_attacks, aes(x = week, y = n_attacks)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = attack_threshold, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(color = escalation_80), size = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "grey", "Escalation" = "red")) +
  labs(
    x = "Week",
    y = "Number of attacks on hospitals",
    title = "Weekly attacks on hospitals in Sudan",
    subtitle = "Escalation defined as weeks with ≥ 4 attacks (≈ 80th percentile)",
    color = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman")

print(escalation_plot_80)

#robustness checks by plotting the percentage of escalation weeks ----
#plot cumulated attacks ----
cumulated_attacks <- ggplot(weekly_attacks, aes(x = n_attacks)) +
  stat_ecdf(geom = "step") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Cumulative distribution of weekly hospital attacks",
    x = "Number of hospital attacks per week",
    y = "Cumulative share of weeks"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman")

print(cumulated_attacks)

cumulated_attacks_zoomed <- ggplot(data = weekly_attacks, aes(x = n_attacks)) +
  stat_ecdf(geom = "step") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:10) +
  labs(
    title = "Cumulative distribution of weekly hospital attacks (zoomed)",
    x = "Number of hospital attacks per week",
    y = "Cumulative share of weeks"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman")

print(cumulated_attacks_zoomed)

#Plotting InsecurityInsight Data -- put attacks on hospitals on map and show number of attacks on the hospitals ----

#plot repeated attacks on hospital on map (data: attacks_locations_sf)

#setting key places for map
key_places <- data.frame(
  name = c("Khartoum", "al-Fashir", "Nyala", "El Geneina"),
  lon = c(32.5599, 25.3500, 24.8800, 22.4400),
  lat = c(15.5007, 13.6300, 12.0500, 13.4500)
)

key_places_sf <- st_as_sf(key_places,
                          coords = c("lon", "lat"),
                          crs = 4326)

# project to same CRS as your map
key_places_sf <- st_transform(key_places_sf, st_crs(sudan_meters))

repeated_attacks_plot <- ggplot() +
  
  geom_sf(data = sudan_meters, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.6) +
  
  geom_sf(
    data = attacks_locations_sf,
    aes(color = n_attacks),
    size = 1.3,
    alpha = 0.85
  ) +
  
  # Key place markers (white circle with black border)
  geom_sf(
    data = key_places_sf,
    shape = 21,
    fill = NA,
    color = "black",
    size = 1.0,
    stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  
  # Labels for key places
  geom_sf_text(
    data = key_places_sf,
    aes(label = name),
    size = 3,
    nudge_y = 60000,
    family = "Times New Roman"
  ) +
  
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    option = "rocket",
    direction = -1,
    name = "Number of attacks"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman") +
  
  labs(
    title = "Repeated attacks on hospitals in Sudan",
    subtitle = "Colors indicate number of attacks at the same location",
    caption = "Yellow: at least 1 attack, Purple: over 40 attacks; Data source: Insecurity Insight"
  )

print(repeated_attacks_plot)

#no plot but table (see how often several hospitals have been attacked)

attacks_per_location <- attacks_coords %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  dplyr::count(x, y, name = "n_attacks") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n_attacks))

head(attacks_per_location, 25)
## # A tibble: 25 × 3
##           x        y n_attacks
##       <dbl>    <dbl>     <int>
##  1 3617883. 1746892.        47
##  2 2816383. 1528365.        40
##  3 3606752. 1758446.        25
##  4 2760723. 1345708.        15
##  5 3617883. 1758446.        15
##  6 3617883. 1735342.         9
##  7 3361849. 1471159.         8
##  8 3629015. 1746892.         7
##  9 2493557. 1505469.         6
## 10 2604876. 1448310.         6
## # ℹ 15 more rows
#Daily attacks on hospitals by RSF and SAF ----
# plot
sudan_hospital_attacks_perp <- sudan_hospital_attacks_data %>%
  filter(`Reported Perpetrator Name` %in% c("Rapid Support Forces", "Sudanese Armed Forces"))

daily_counts <- sudan_hospital_attacks_perp %>%
  count(event_date, `Reported Perpetrator Name`)


p5 <- ggplot(daily_counts,
       aes(x = event_date, 
           y = n, 
           color = `Reported Perpetrator Name`)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Daily Attacks on hospitals: RSF vs SAF",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Number of Attacks",
    scale_color_manual(
      name = "Perpetrator",
      values = c(
        "SAF" = "#B22222",
        "RSF" = "#1F4E79",
        "RSF + SAF" = "#7A1FA2" )
  )) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman")

print(p5)

#Who attacks where and what?
#filter for reported perpetrator and geometry
sudan_hospital_attacks_actor_sf <- sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>%
  dplyr::select(
    actor = `Reported Perpetrator Name`,
    geometry
  )

#exclude NAs and NIs
sudan_hospital_attacks_actor_sf_filtered <- sudan_hospital_attacks_actor_sf %>%
  mutate(
    actor_clean = case_when(
      actor == "Sudanese Armed Forces" ~ "SAF",
      actor == "Rapid Support Forces" ~ "RSF",
      actor %in% c(
        "Rapid Support Forces, Sudanese Armed Forces",
        "Sudanese Armed Forces, Rapid Support Forces"
      ) ~ "RSF + SAF",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(actor_clean))

# plot with key places and attacks per actor 

actor_attacks_plot_color_new <- ggplot() +
  
  geom_sf(
    data = sudan_meters,
    fill = NA,
    color = "black",
    size = 0.6
  ) +
  
  geom_sf(
    data = sudan_hospital_attacks_actor_sf_filtered,
    aes(color = actor_clean),
    size = 1.3,
    alpha = 0.85
  ) +
  
  # Key place markers (transparent circle with black border)
  geom_sf(
    data = key_places_sf,
    shape = 21,
    fill = NA,
    color = "black",
    size = 1,
    stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  
  # Labels for key places
  geom_sf_text(
    data = key_places_sf,
    aes(label = name),
    size = 3,
    nudge_y = 60000,
    family = "Times New Roman"
  ) +
  
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "Reported perpetrator",
    values = c(
      "SAF" = "#B22222",
      "RSF" = "#1F4E79",
      "RSF + SAF" = "#7A1FA2"
    ),
    breaks = c("SAF", "RSF", "RSF + SAF")
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman") +
  
  labs(
    title = "Attacks on hospitals in Sudan by conflict party",
    subtitle = "Red: SAF, Blue: RSF, Purple: jointly reported (RSF + SAF)",
    caption = "Data source: Insecurity Insight"
  )

print(actor_attacks_plot_color_new)

#with grey dots for no NIs
actor_attacks_plot_color <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(
    data = sudan_meters,
    fill = NA,
    color = "black",
    size = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = sudan_hospital_attacks_actor_sf,
    aes(color = actor),
    size = 1.3,
    alpha = 0.85
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "Perpetrator",
    values = c(
      "Sudanese Armed Forces" = "#C00000",  
      "Rapid Support Forces" = "#1F4E79"    
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman") +
  labs(
    title = "Attacks on hospitals in Sudan by conflict party",
    subtitle = "Red: SAF, Blue: RSF",
    caption = "Data source: Insecurity Insight"
  )

print(actor_attacks_plot_color)

# Weapons and actors ----
#which weapons are used? ----
weapons_overall <- sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  dplyr::count(`Weapon Carried/Used`, name = "n_attacks") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n_attacks))

#in percentage
weapons_overall <- weapons_overall %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    share = round(n_attacks / sum(n_attacks) * 100, 1)
  )
weapons_overall
## # A tibble: 15 × 3
##    `Weapon Carried/Used`                        n_attacks share
##    <chr>                                            <int> <dbl>
##  1 Firearms                                           123  48.2
##  2 Shelling                                            39  15.3
##  3 Aerial Bomb: Plane                                  22   8.6
##  4 Unspecified Explosive                               15   5.9
##  5 Artillery                                           14   5.5
##  6 Aerial Bomb: Drone                                   9   3.5
##  7 Arson                                                9   3.5
##  8 No Information on the Weapon Used                    9   3.5
##  9 Mortar                                               4   1.6
## 10 Fist and Foot                                        3   1.2
## 11 Missile                                              3   1.2
## 12 Rocket                                               2   0.8
## 13 Aerial Bomb: Missile                                 1   0.4
## 14 Aerial Bomb: Plane, Artillery                        1   0.4
## 15 Artillery, No Information on the Weapon Used         1   0.4
#group weapons, to see which actor uses which weapons
weapons_grouped <- sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    weapon_group = dplyr::case_when(
      
      `Weapon Carried/Used` %in% c(
        "Shelling", "Artillery", "Mortar", "Rocket",
        "Missile", "Unspecified Explosive"
      ) ~ "Explosives / Heavy fire",
      
      `Weapon Carried/Used` %in% c(
        "Aerial Bomb: Plane", "Aerial Bomb: Drone",
        "Aerial Bomb: Missile", "Aerial Bomb: Plane, Artillery"
      ) ~ "Air-delivered weapons",
      
      `Weapon Carried/Used` == "Firearms" ~ "Firearms",
      
      `Weapon Carried/Used` == "Arson" ~ "Arson",
      
      `Weapon Carried/Used` == "Fist and Foot" ~ "Physical force",
      
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::count(weapon_group, name = "n_attacks") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    share = round(n_attacks / sum(n_attacks) * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n_attacks))

weapons_grouped
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   weapon_group            n_attacks share
##   <chr>                       <int> <dbl>
## 1 Firearms                      123  48.2
## 2 Explosives / Heavy fire        77  30.2
## 3 Air-delivered weapons          33  12.9
## 4 Unknown                        10   3.9
## 5 Arson                           9   3.5
## 6 Physical force                  3   1.2
#Add type of actor (RSF & SAF x type of weapon)----
attacks_actor_weapon <- sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    
    actor_group = dplyr::case_when(
      grepl("Rapid Support Forces|RSF", `Reported Perpetrator Name`, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "RSF",
      grepl("Sudanese Armed Forces|SAF|Sudan Armed Forces", `Reported Perpetrator Name`, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "SAF",
      grepl("Police|Security", `Reported Perpetrator Name`, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "State (other)",
      grepl("Unknown|Unidentified", `Reported Perpetrator Name`, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Unknown",
      TRUE ~ "Other armed actors"
    ),
    
    weapon_group = dplyr::case_when(
      `Weapon Carried/Used` %in% c(
        "Shelling", "Artillery", "Mortar", "Rocket",
        "Missile", "Unspecified Explosive"
      ) ~ "Explosives / Heavy fire",
      
      `Weapon Carried/Used` %in% c(
        "Aerial Bomb: Plane", "Aerial Bomb: Drone",
        "Aerial Bomb: Missile", "Aerial Bomb: Plane, Artillery"
      ) ~ "Air-delivered weapons",
      
      `Weapon Carried/Used` == "Firearms" ~ "Firearms",
      `Weapon Carried/Used` == "Arson" ~ "Arson",
      `Weapon Carried/Used` == "Fist and Foot" ~ "Physical force",
      
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    )
  )

actor_weapon_counts <- attacks_actor_weapon %>%
  dplyr::count(actor_group, weapon_group, name = "n_attacks") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n_attacks))

#Compare RSF and SAF, wepaon shares, incl. heatmap
actor_weapon_shares <- actor_weapon_counts %>%
  dplyr::group_by(actor_group) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    share_actor = round(n_attacks / sum(n_attacks) * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

actor_weapon_shares
## # A tibble: 16 × 4
##    actor_group        weapon_group            n_attacks share_actor
##    <chr>              <chr>                       <int>       <dbl>
##  1 RSF                Firearms                       93        51.7
##  2 RSF                Explosives / Heavy fire        64        35.6
##  3 SAF                Air-delivered weapons          23        56.1
##  4 Other armed actors Firearms                       11        52.4
##  5 RSF                Air-delivered weapons          10         5.6
##  6 Unknown            Firearms                       10        76.9
##  7 SAF                Firearms                        9        22  
##  8 Other armed actors Explosives / Heavy fire         6        28.6
##  9 RSF                Arson                           6         3.3
## 10 SAF                Unknown                         5        12.2
## 11 RSF                Unknown                         4         2.2
## 12 SAF                Explosives / Heavy fire         4         9.8
## 13 Other armed actors Arson                           3        14.3
## 14 RSF                Physical force                  3         1.7
## 15 Unknown            Explosives / Heavy fire         3        23.1
## 16 Other armed actors Unknown                         1         4.8
#heatmap for who x what
heatmap_weapon_shares <- ggplot(actor_weapon_shares,
       aes(x = weapon_group, y = actor_group, fill = share_actor)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    option = "rocket",
    direction = -1,
    name = "Share of attacks (%)"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Weapon category",
    y = "Actor",
    title = "Weapons used in attacks on hospitals by actor"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

print(heatmap_weapon_shares)

#aggregate attacks per week 
attacks_weekly <- sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    week = floor_date(Date, unit = "week", week_start = 1)
  ) %>%
  count(week, name = "n_attacks")

#Show: actors and attacks in plot ----
sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>% st_drop_geometry () %>% count (`Reported Perpetrator Name`, sort = TRUE)
## # A tibble: 6 × 2
##   `Reported Perpetrator Name`                     n
##   <chr>                                       <int>
## 1 Rapid Support Forces                          165
## 2 Sudanese Armed Forces                          41
## 3 No Information                                 20
## 4 Rapid Support Forces, Sudanese Armed Forces    15
## 5 Unidentified Armed Actor                       13
## 6 South Sudan People's Defence Force              1
attacks_weekly_actor <- sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    week = floor_date(Date, unit = "week", week_start = 1),
    actor_group = case_when(
      grepl("Rapid Support Forces",`Reported Perpetrator Name` , ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "RSF",
      grepl("Sudanese Armed Forces", `Reported Perpetrator Name`, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "SAF",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  count(week, actor_group)
#plot it
plot3 <- ggplot(
  attacks_weekly_actor,
  aes(x = week, y = n, color = actor_group)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "RSF"   = "#7B1E1E",
      "SAF"   = "#1F4E79",  
      "Other" = "grey60"  
    ),
    breaks = c("RSF", "SAF", "Other")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Week",
    y = "Number of attacks",
    color = "Actor"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman")

print(plot3)

Merge all data (ZINB preparation)

#merge all data
#do we have all we need? check:

#all grid-week combinations: 
# panel_df
#all hospital attacks per grid week:
# hospital_attacks_grid_week
#all territorial counts:
# territorial_counts
#conflict intensity per grid-week:
# conflict_intensity_grid_week
#population per grid:
# population_grid

#make sure to have the same time period of the data
# Check min / max week in each Data-Set
range(hospital_attacks_grid_week$week, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] "2023-03-26" "2025-06-22"
range(territorial_counts$week, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] "2020-05-31" "2024-11-03"
range(conflict_intensity_grid_week$week, na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] "2023-01-01" "2024-11-03"
start_week <- as.Date("2023-03-26")  # latest common start
end_week   <- as.Date("2024-11-03")  # earliest common end

hospital_attacks_grid_week <- hospital_attacks_grid_week %>%
  dplyr::filter(week >= start_week, week <= end_week)

territorial_counts <- territorial_counts %>%
  dplyr::filter(week >= start_week, week <= end_week)

conflict_intensity_grid_week <- conflict_intensity_grid_week %>%
  dplyr::filter(week >= start_week, week <= end_week)

#check if correctly filtered
range(hospital_attacks_grid_week$week)
## [1] "2023-03-26" "2024-11-03"
range(territorial_counts$week)
## [1] "2023-04-09" "2024-11-03"
range(conflict_intensity_grid_week$week)
## [1] "2023-03-26" "2024-11-03"
#check numbers of weeks per set
Reduce(
  intersect,
  list(
    unique(hospital_attacks_grid_week$week),
    unique(territorial_counts$week),
    unique(conflict_intensity_grid_week$week)
  )
) |> length()
## [1] 55
#correct the weeeks of territorial counts
all_weeks <- seq(
  from = as.Date("2023-03-26"),
  to   = as.Date("2024-11-03"),
  by   = "week"
)

territorial_counts <- territorial_counts %>%
  tidyr::complete(
    grid_id,
    week = all_weeks,
    fill = list(n_territorial_events = 0)
  )

range(hospital_attacks_grid_week$week)
## [1] "2023-03-26" "2024-11-03"
range(territorial_counts$week)
## [1] "2023-03-26" "2024-11-03"
range(conflict_intensity_grid_week$week)
## [1] "2023-03-26" "2024-11-03"
#check: same keys? 
lapply(
  list(
    panel_df,
    hospital_attacks_grid_week,
    territorial_counts,
    conflict_intensity_grid_week
  ),
  function(x) names(x)
)
## [[1]]
## [1] "grid_id" "week"   
## 
## [[2]]
## [1] "grid_id"          "week"             "hospital_attacks"
## 
## [[3]]
## [1] "grid_id"              "week"                 "n_territorial_events"
## 
## [[4]]
## [1] "grid_id"            "week"               "conflict_intensity"
#merge (left joins, no inner join or we lose structural zeros)
panel_master <- panel_df %>%
  left_join(
    hospital_attacks_grid_week,
    by = c("grid_id", "week")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    territorial_counts,
    by = c("grid_id", "week")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    conflict_intensity_grid_week,
    by = c("grid_id", "week")
  )
#transform NAs to 0, for ZINB essential
panel_master <- panel_master %>%
  mutate(
    hospital_attacks = ifelse(is.na(hospital_attacks), 0, hospital_attacks),
    n_territorial_events = ifelse(is.na(n_territorial_events), 0, n_territorial_events),
    conflict_intensity = ifelse(is.na(conflict_intensity), 0, conflict_intensity)
  )
#sanity and mandatory checks
summary(panel_master$hospital_attacks)
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
##  0.000000  0.000000  0.000000  0.001024  0.000000 19.000000
mean(panel_master$hospital_attacks)
## [1] 0.00102424
#mean is almost zero: 0.0005526495
#check logic
panel_master %>%
  dplyr::filter(hospital_attacks > 0) %>%
  dplyr::select(grid_id, week, hospital_attacks, conflict_intensity, n_territorial_events) %>%
  head(10)
##    grid_id       week hospital_attacks conflict_intensity n_territorial_events
## 1     1515 2024-11-03                1                  1                    0
## 2     1165 2024-10-27                1                  0                    0
## 3     1515 2024-10-20                1                 53                    6
## 4     1640 2024-10-20                1                  3                    0
## 5     1165 2024-10-13                1                  1                    0
## 6     1452 2024-10-13                1                  2                    0
## 7     1640 2024-10-13                1                  4                    0
## 8     1437 2024-09-29                1                  0                    0
## 9     1704 2024-09-29                1                 71                   16
## 10    1165 2024-08-25                2                  0                    0
#check wether it happens often that hosptial attack = 1 and rest =0
hospital_only <- panel_master %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    hospital_attacks > 0,
    conflict_intensity == 0,
    n_territorial_events == 0
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(grid_id, week, hospital_attacks, conflict_intensity, n_territorial_events)

nrow(hospital_only)
## [1] 51
#share of hospital attack grid-weeks (without something else happening)
total_hospital_weeks <- panel_master %>%
  filter(hospital_attacks > 0) %>%
  nrow()

nrow(hospital_only) / total_hospital_weeks
## [1] 0.3923077
#share of hospital attacks with conflict in grid-week
hospital_with_conflict <- panel_master %>%
  filter(
    hospital_attacks > 0,
    conflict_intensity > 0
  )
nrow(hospital_with_conflict)
## [1] 79
nrow(hospital_only)
## [1] 51
#share of hospital attacks and territorial changes in grid-week
hospital_with_territorial <- panel_master %>%
  filter(
    hospital_attacks > 0,
    n_territorial_events > 0
  )

nrow(hospital_with_territorial)
## [1] 21
nrow(hospital_only)
## [1] 51
#and share of both 
hospital_with_territorial_conflict <- panel_master %>%
  filter(
    hospital_attacks > 0,
    n_territorial_events > 0, 
    conflict_intensity > 0
  )

nrow(hospital_with_territorial_conflict)
## [1] 21
#testing things to check whether merge was correct
sum(conflict_intensity_grid_week$conflict_intensity)
## [1] 9461
nrow(conflict_intensity_clean)
## [1] 9657
nrow(conflict_intensity_grid_week)
## [1] 2461
#creating lags - because attacks do not (or do??) occur time-delayed
panel_master_lag <- panel_master %>%
  arrange(grid_id, week) %>%
  group_by(grid_id) %>%
  mutate(
    conflict_intensity_lag1 = lag(conflict_intensity, 1),
    territorial_events_lag1 = lag(n_territorial_events, 1)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
#sanity check
summary(panel_master_lag$conflict_intensity_lag1)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
##  0.0000  0.0000  0.0000  0.0357  0.0000 93.0000    2424
summary(panel_master_lag$territorial_events_lag1)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
##  0.0000  0.0000  0.0000  0.0027  0.0000 16.0000    2424
#check share: Lag share - Did something happen before (in grid), that might have influenced the attack? 
panel_master_lag %>%
  filter(hospital_attacks > 0) %>%
  summarise(
    share_conflict_lag1 = mean(conflict_intensity_lag1 > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_territorial_lag1 = mean(territorial_events_lag1 > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 2
##   share_conflict_lag1 share_territorial_lag1
##                 <dbl>                  <dbl>
## 1               0.543                  0.101
#check share without lag: Contemporaneous Share - Does something happen in the same grid-week?
panel_master %>%
  filter(hospital_attacks > 0) %>%
  summarise(
    share_conflict_now = mean(conflict_intensity > 0),
    share_territorial_now = mean(n_territorial_events > 0)
  )
##   share_conflict_now share_territorial_now
## 1          0.6076923             0.1615385
#visualization of repeated attacks in one grid 
# point at the repeated-attack coordinate
pt47 <- st_as_sf(
  data.frame(x = 3617883, y = 1746892),
  coords = c("x","y"),
  crs = st_crs(sudan_meters)   # same metric CRS
)

# get the grid cell (and its id) that contains the point
grid47 <- st_join(pt47, grid["grid_id"], join = st_within)

grid_id_47 <- grid47$grid_id[1]
grid_id_47
## [1] 1640
grid404_plot <- panel_master_lag %>%
  filter(grid_id == grid_id_47) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = hospital_attacks)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly hospital attacks in the grid containing the most repeated coordinates",
    subtitle = paste0("Grid: ", grid_id_47, " | Location: (3617883, 1746892)"),
    x = "Week",
    y = "Number of hospital attacks"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman")

print(grid404_plot)

#merge population grid on panel_master_lag ----
population_grid_clean <- population_grid %>%
  rename(grid_id = ID)

panel_master_lag <- panel_master_lag %>%
  left_join(population_grid_clean, by = "grid_id")

sum(is.na(panel_master_lag$population_2023))
## [1] 14268
#loose grids where population = 0 
panel_master_lag <- panel_master_lag %>%
  mutate(
    population_2023 = as.numeric(unlist(population_2023))
  )

panel_master_lag <- panel_master_lag %>%
  mutate(
    population = ifelse(is.na(population_2023) | is.nan(population_2023), 0, population_2023)
  )

panel_master_lag <- panel_master_lag %>%
  filter(population > 0)

#check
str(panel_master_lag$population)
##  num [1:196620] 0.287 0.287 0.287 0.287 0.287 ...
table(panel_master_lag$population == 0)
## 
##  FALSE 
## 196620
panel_master_lag <- panel_master_lag %>%
  mutate(log_population = log(population))

ZINB analysis

#Zero-Inflated Negative Binomial Regression 

#ZINB_pop ----
zinb_pop <- zeroinfl(
  hospital_attacks ~ 
    territorial_events_lag1 +
    conflict_intensity + 
    n_territorial_events +
    log_population |
    conflict_intensity +
    log_population + 
    territorial_events_lag1 +
    n_territorial_events,
  data = panel_master_lag,
  dist = "negbin"
)

summary(zinb_pop)
## 
## Call:
## zeroinfl(formula = hospital_attacks ~ territorial_events_lag1 + conflict_intensity + 
##     n_territorial_events + log_population | conflict_intensity + log_population + 
##     territorial_events_lag1 + n_territorial_events, data = panel_master_lag, 
##     dist = "negbin")
## 
## Pearson residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
##  -0.43049  -0.01526  -0.01445  -0.01340 173.78934 
## 
## Count model coefficients (negbin with log link):
##                          Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)             -0.720459   0.500573  -1.439   0.1501    
## territorial_events_lag1  0.009295   0.059897   0.155   0.8767    
## conflict_intensity       0.014477   0.008708   1.662   0.0964 .  
## n_territorial_events    -0.024906   0.067795  -0.367   0.7133    
## log_population          -0.129104   0.073069  -1.767   0.0772 .  
## Log(theta)              -1.523885   0.234977  -6.485 8.86e-11 ***
## 
## Zero-inflation model coefficients (binomial with logit link):
##                         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)              6.83487    0.44732  15.280   <2e-16 ***
## conflict_intensity      -1.95855    0.23449  -8.352   <2e-16 ***
## log_population          -0.10652    0.06512  -1.636   0.1019    
## territorial_events_lag1 -0.64184    0.28482  -2.253   0.0242 *  
## n_territorial_events     0.13680    0.54691   0.250   0.8025    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 
## 
## Theta = 0.2179 
## Number of iterations in BFGS optimization: 26 
## Log-likelihood: -862.2 on 11 Df
zinb_pop_lag <- zeroinfl(
  hospital_attacks ~ 
    conflict_intensity_lag1 +
    territorial_events_lag1 +
    n_territorial_events +
    log_population |
    conflict_intensity_lag1 +
    log_population + 
    territorial_events_lag1 +
    n_territorial_events,
  data = panel_master_lag,
  dist = "negbin"
)

summary(zinb_pop_lag)
## 
## Call:
## zeroinfl(formula = hospital_attacks ~ conflict_intensity_lag1 + territorial_events_lag1 + 
##     n_territorial_events + log_population | conflict_intensity_lag1 + 
##     log_population + territorial_events_lag1 + n_territorial_events, 
##     data = panel_master_lag, dist = "negbin")
## 
## Pearson residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
##  -0.45560  -0.01635  -0.01525  -0.01374 528.51278 
## 
## Count model coefficients (negbin with log link):
##                          Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)             -0.198438   0.504233  -0.394   0.6939    
## conflict_intensity_lag1  0.006799   0.007419   0.916   0.3594    
## territorial_events_lag1  0.013204   0.059999   0.220   0.8258    
## n_territorial_events    -0.027243   0.057762  -0.472   0.6372    
## log_population          -0.171451   0.076971  -2.227   0.0259 *  
## Log(theta)              -1.303588   0.234441  -5.560 2.69e-08 ***
## 
## Zero-inflation model coefficients (binomial with logit link):
##                         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)              7.15266    0.42985  16.640  < 2e-16 ***
## conflict_intensity_lag1 -1.46404    0.15554  -9.413  < 2e-16 ***
## log_population          -0.13940    0.06507  -2.142 0.032167 *  
## territorial_events_lag1  0.82810    0.36116   2.293 0.021855 *  
## n_territorial_events    -1.45086    0.44046  -3.294 0.000988 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 
## 
## Theta = 0.2716 
## Number of iterations in BFGS optimization: 37 
## Log-likelihood: -883.7 on 11 Df
#actor specific ZINBs ----
#first: add actor specifications in panel_master_lag ----
hospital_attacks_actor <- sudan_hospital_attacks_sf %>%
  mutate(
    RSF = grepl("Rapid Support Forces", `Reported Perpetrator Name`, ignore.case = TRUE),
    SAF = grepl("Sudanese Armed Forces", `Reported Perpetrator Name`, ignore.case = TRUE)
  )

hospital_attacks_actor <- st_join(
  hospital_attacks_actor,
  grid_sudan %>% dplyr::select(grid_id),
  join = st_intersects
)
sum(is.na(hospital_attacks_actor$grid_id))
## [1] 0
#aggregation grid-week-actor
attacks_gridweek <- hospital_attacks_actor %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(grid_id, week) %>%
  summarise(
    hospital_attacks_RSF = sum(RSF, na.rm = TRUE),
    hospital_attacks_SAF = sum(SAF, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

panel_master_lag_actors <- panel_master_lag %>%
  left_join(attacks_gridweek, by = c("grid_id", "week")) %>%
  mutate(
    hospital_attacks_RSF = ifelse(is.na(hospital_attacks_RSF), 0, hospital_attacks_RSF),
    hospital_attacks_SAF = ifelse(is.na(hospital_attacks_SAF), 0, hospital_attacks_SAF)
  )

#second: add territorial losses 
territorial_losses <- territorial_events_sf %>%
  mutate(
    loss_SAF = sub_event_type == "Non-state actor overtakes territory",
    loss_RSF = sub_event_type == "Government regains territory",
    loss_nonviolent = sub_event_type == "Non-violent transfer of territory"
  )
#put to grids
territorial_losses <- st_join(
  territorial_losses,
  grid_sudan %>% dplyr::select(grid_id),
  join = st_intersects
)
#aggregate on grid-week
territorial_losses_gridweek <- territorial_losses %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(grid_id, week) %>%
  summarise(
    territorial_losses_RSF = sum(loss_RSF, na.rm = TRUE),
    territorial_losses_SAF = sum(loss_SAF, na.rm = TRUE),
    territorial_transfers_nonviolent = sum(loss_nonviolent, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
#merge in panel
panel_master_lag_actors <- panel_master_lag_actors %>%
  left_join(territorial_losses_gridweek, by = c("grid_id", "week")) %>%
  mutate(
    territorial_losses_RSF = ifelse(is.na(territorial_losses_RSF), 0, territorial_losses_RSF),
    territorial_losses_SAF = ifelse(is.na(territorial_losses_SAF), 0, territorial_losses_SAF),
    territorial_transfers_nonviolent = ifelse(is.na(territorial_transfers_nonviolent), 0,
                                              territorial_transfers_nonviolent)
  )

#create lags for territorial losses
panel_master_lag_actors <- panel_master_lag_actors %>%
  arrange(grid_id, week) %>%
  group_by(grid_id) %>%
  mutate(
    territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 = lag(territorial_losses_RSF, 1),
    territorial_losses_SAF_lag1 = lag(territorial_losses_SAF, 1)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
#sanity check
summary(panel_master_lag_actors$territorial_losses_RSF_lag1)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
##   0.000   0.000   0.000   0.001   0.000  14.000    2260
summary(panel_master_lag_actors$territorial_losses_SAF_lag1)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
##  0.0000  0.0000  0.0000  0.0009  0.0000 14.0000    2260
#check share: Lag share - Did something happen before (in grid), that might have influenced the attack? 
panel_master_lag_actors %>%
  filter(hospital_attacks_RSF > 0) %>%
  summarise(
    share_conflict_lag1 = mean(conflict_intensity_lag1 > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_territorial_lag1 = mean(territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 2
##   share_conflict_lag1 share_territorial_lag1
##                 <dbl>                  <dbl>
## 1               0.483                 0.0508
#ZINB_RSF ----

#zinb_RSF - contemporaneous
# RSF – contemporaneous
zinb_RSF_t <- zeroinfl(
  hospital_attacks_RSF ~
    conflict_intensity +
    territorial_losses_RSF +
    territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 +
    log_population |
    conflict_intensity +
    territorial_losses_RSF +
    territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 +
    log_population,
  data = panel_master_lag_actors,
  dist = "negbin"
)

summary(zinb_RSF_t)
## 
## Call:
## zeroinfl(formula = hospital_attacks_RSF ~ conflict_intensity + territorial_losses_RSF + 
##     territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 + log_population | conflict_intensity + 
##     territorial_losses_RSF + territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 + log_population, 
##     data = panel_master_lag_actors, dist = "negbin")
## 
## Pearson residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
##  -0.43812  -0.01638  -0.01544  -0.01403 121.66496 
## 
## Count model coefficients (negbin with log link):
##                              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                 -0.593000   0.649079  -0.914   0.3609    
## conflict_intensity           0.015622   0.008862   1.763   0.0780 .  
## territorial_losses_RSF      -0.011683   0.121877  -0.096   0.9236    
## territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 -0.193943   0.141128  -1.374   0.1694    
## log_population              -0.203047   0.107392  -1.891   0.0587 .  
## Log(theta)                  -1.438490   0.263704  -5.455  4.9e-08 ***
## 
## Zero-inflation model coefficients (binomial with logit link):
##                             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                  6.23822    0.50413  12.374  < 2e-16 ***
## conflict_intensity          -1.78174    0.21833  -8.161 3.33e-16 ***
## territorial_losses_RSF       8.46564    1.43602   5.895 3.74e-09 ***
## territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 -2.40868    0.98647  -2.442   0.0146 *  
## log_population              -0.05903    0.08765  -0.673   0.5006    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 
## 
## Theta = 0.2373 
## Number of iterations in BFGS optimization: 50 
## Log-likelihood: -833.7 on 11 Df
# RSF – lagged
zinb_RSF_lag <- zeroinfl(
  hospital_attacks_RSF ~
    conflict_intensity_lag1 +
    territorial_losses_RSF +
    territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 +
    log_population |
    conflict_intensity_lag1 +
    territorial_losses_RSF +
    territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 +
    log_population,
  data = panel_master_lag_actors,
  dist = "negbin"
)

summary(zinb_RSF_lag)
## 
## Call:
## zeroinfl(formula = hospital_attacks_RSF ~ conflict_intensity_lag1 + territorial_losses_RSF + 
##     territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 + log_population | conflict_intensity_lag1 + 
##     territorial_losses_RSF + territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 + log_population, 
##     data = panel_master_lag_actors, dist = "negbin")
## 
## Pearson residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
##  -0.41264  -0.01678  -0.01619  -0.01484 572.58279 
## 
## Count model coefficients (negbin with log link):
##                               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                 -0.4534846  0.7062160  -0.642   0.5208    
## conflict_intensity_lag1      0.0123655  0.0085298   1.450   0.1471    
## territorial_losses_RSF       0.0001404  0.1163828   0.001   0.9990    
## territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 -0.1269677  0.1440543  -0.881   0.3781    
## log_population              -0.2362438  0.1161411  -2.034   0.0419 *  
## Log(theta)                  -1.5215996  0.2891337  -5.263 1.42e-07 ***
## 
## Zero-inflation model coefficients (binomial with logit link):
##                             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                  6.24936    0.52958  11.801  < 2e-16 ***
## conflict_intensity_lag1     -1.71331    0.28472  -6.018 1.77e-09 ***
## territorial_losses_RSF       5.33240    1.48064   3.601 0.000317 ***
## territorial_losses_RSF_lag1  3.09677    2.41058   1.285 0.198913    
## log_population              -0.08749    0.09164  -0.955 0.339708    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 
## 
## Theta = 0.2184 
## Number of iterations in BFGS optimization: 57 
## Log-likelihood: -861.6 on 11 Df
#model fit? 
AIC(zinb_RSF_t, zinb_RSF_lag)
##              df      AIC
## zinb_RSF_t   11 1689.345
## zinb_RSF_lag 11 1745.244
#ZINB_SAF ---- 
#zinb SAF contemporaneous 
zinb_SAF_t <- zeroinfl(
  hospital_attacks_SAF ~
    conflict_intensity +
    territorial_losses_SAF +
    log_population |
    conflict_intensity +
    territorial_losses_SAF +
    log_population,
  data = panel_master_lag_actors,
  dist = "negbin"
)

summary(zinb_SAF_t)
## 
## Call:
## zeroinfl(formula = hospital_attacks_SAF ~ conflict_intensity + territorial_losses_SAF + 
##     log_population | conflict_intensity + territorial_losses_SAF + log_population, 
##     data = panel_master_lag_actors, dist = "negbin")
## 
## Pearson residuals:
##        Min         1Q     Median         3Q        Max 
##  -0.268436  -0.011479  -0.009228  -0.007299 142.902806 
## 
## Count model coefficients (negbin with log link):
##                        Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)            -2.90089    0.98327  -2.950  0.00318 ** 
## conflict_intensity      0.01924    0.01366   1.409  0.15892    
## territorial_losses_SAF  0.05621    0.31502   0.178  0.85839    
## log_population         -0.01293    0.13221  -0.098  0.92207    
## Log(theta)             -2.42849    0.45579  -5.328 9.93e-08 ***
## 
## Zero-inflation model coefficients (binomial with logit link):
##                        Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)              7.0399     0.9888   7.120 1.08e-12 ***
## conflict_intensity      -1.5122     0.2783  -5.433 5.53e-08 ***
## territorial_losses_SAF   5.8158     3.0028   1.937   0.0528 .  
## log_population          -0.1717     0.1268  -1.354   0.1757    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 
## 
## Theta = 0.0882 
## Number of iterations in BFGS optimization: 53 
## Log-likelihood: -331.4 on 9 Df
# SAF – lagged
zinb_SAF_lag <- zeroinfl(
  hospital_attacks_SAF ~
    conflict_intensity_lag1 +
    territorial_losses_SAF +
    log_population |
    conflict_intensity_lag1 +
    territorial_losses_SAF +
    log_population,
  data = panel_master_lag_actors,
  dist = "negbin"
)

summary(zinb_SAF_lag)
## 
## Call:
## zeroinfl(formula = hospital_attacks_SAF ~ conflict_intensity_lag1 + territorial_losses_SAF + 
##     log_population | conflict_intensity_lag1 + territorial_losses_SAF + 
##     log_population, data = panel_master_lag_actors, dist = "negbin")
## 
## Pearson residuals:
##        Min         1Q     Median         3Q        Max 
##  -0.231839  -0.011936  -0.009275  -0.006934 202.380406 
## 
## Count model coefficients (negbin with log link):
##                         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)             -2.08242    1.00713  -2.068   0.0387 *  
## conflict_intensity_lag1  0.01270    0.01301   0.976   0.3290    
## territorial_losses_SAF  -0.13050    0.26356  -0.495   0.6205    
## log_population          -0.14131    0.14459  -0.977   0.3284    
## Log(theta)              -2.67194    0.44211  -6.044 1.51e-09 ***
## 
## Zero-inflation model coefficients (binomial with logit link):
##                         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)               7.6159     0.9683   7.866 3.68e-15 ***
## conflict_intensity_lag1  -1.2438     0.2229  -5.579 2.42e-08 ***
## territorial_losses_SAF   -8.5163   149.3100  -0.057   0.9545    
## log_population           -0.2769     0.1351  -2.050   0.0404 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 
## 
## Theta = 0.0691 
## Number of iterations in BFGS optimization: 58 
## Log-likelihood: -331.2 on 9 Df

Statistical and robustness checks

## robustness and statistical checks 

## multicollinearity ----

#collinearity check for ZINB base model ----
vif_res <- check_collinearity(
  glm(
    hospital_attacks ~ territorial_events_lag1 + 
      n_territorial_events + 
      conflict_intensity +
      log_population,
    data = panel_master_lag,
    family = poisson()
  )
)

vif_df <- as.data.frame(vif_res)

names(vif_df)
## [1] "Term"              "VIF"               "VIF_CI_low"       
## [4] "VIF_CI_high"       "SE_factor"         "Tolerance"        
## [7] "Tolerance_CI_low"  "Tolerance_CI_high"
vif_df
##                      Term      VIF VIF_CI_low VIF_CI_high SE_factor Tolerance
## 1 territorial_events_lag1 1.442169   1.433871    1.450626  1.200904 0.6933999
## 2    n_territorial_events 1.382159   1.374395    1.390083  1.175653 0.7235059
## 3      conflict_intensity 1.784584   1.773245    1.796089  1.335883 0.5603548
## 4          log_population 1.087285   1.082216    1.092665  1.042729 0.9197225
##   Tolerance_CI_low Tolerance_CI_high
## 1        0.6893575         0.6974128
## 2        0.7193814         0.7275927
## 3        0.5567654         0.5639379
## 4        0.9151932         0.9240300
vif_df <- vif_df %>% rename(Parameter = Term)
vif_df
##                 Parameter      VIF VIF_CI_low VIF_CI_high SE_factor Tolerance
## 1 territorial_events_lag1 1.442169   1.433871    1.450626  1.200904 0.6933999
## 2    n_territorial_events 1.382159   1.374395    1.390083  1.175653 0.7235059
## 3      conflict_intensity 1.784584   1.773245    1.796089  1.335883 0.5603548
## 4          log_population 1.087285   1.082216    1.092665  1.042729 0.9197225
##   Tolerance_CI_low Tolerance_CI_high
## 1        0.6893575         0.6974128
## 2        0.7193814         0.7275927
## 3        0.5567654         0.5639379
## 4        0.9151932         0.9240300
#plot for multi collinearity 
multicollinearity_plot <- ggplot(vif_df, aes(x = reorder(Parameter, VIF), y = VIF)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#1F4E79") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 10, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Variance Inflation Factor (VIF)",
    title = "Assessment of multicollinearity",
    subtitle = "All VIF values are well below conventional thresholds"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman")

print(multicollinearity_plot)

ggsave(
  filename = "multicollinearity_plot.png",
  plot = multicollinearity_plot,    
  width = 18,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm", dpi = 300
)

#collinearity check for ZINB RSF model  -----
vif_RSF <- check_collinearity(
  glm(
    hospital_attacks_RSF ~
      conflict_intensity +
      territorial_losses_RSF +
      territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 +
      log_population,
    data = panel_master_lag_actors,
    family = poisson()
  )
)

vif_RSF_df <- as.data.frame(vif_RSF) %>%
  rename(Parameter = Term)

ggplot(vif_RSF_df, aes(x = reorder(Parameter, VIF), y = VIF)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#8B0000") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 10, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Variance Inflation Factor (VIF)",
    title = "Assessment of multicollinearity (RSF model)",
    subtitle = "No evidence of problematic multicollinearity"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman")

#collinearity check for ZINB SAF model  -----
vif_SAF <- check_collinearity(
  glm(
    hospital_attacks_SAF ~
      conflict_intensity +
      territorial_losses_SAF +
      log_population,
    data = panel_master_lag_actors,
    family = poisson()
  )
)

vif_SAF_df <- as.data.frame(vif_SAF) %>%
  rename(Parameter = Term)

ggplot(vif_SAF_df, aes(x = reorder(Parameter, VIF), y = VIF)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#1F4E79") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 10, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Variance Inflation Factor (VIF)",
    title = "Assessment of multicollinearity (SAF model)",
    subtitle = "All VIF values are below conventional thresholds"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Times New Roman")

#predict comparable models ----
# Poisson ----
pois_RSF <- glm(
  hospital_attacks_RSF ~
    conflict_intensity +
    territorial_losses_RSF +
    territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 +
    log_population,
  data = panel_master_lag_actors,
  family = poisson()
)

summary(pois_RSF)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = hospital_attacks_RSF ~ conflict_intensity + territorial_losses_RSF + 
##     territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 + log_population, family = poisson(), 
##     data = panel_master_lag_actors)
## 
## Deviance Residuals: 
##     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  
## -2.1903  -0.0435  -0.0386  -0.0344  17.8450  
## 
## Coefficients:
##                              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                 -6.650530   0.154961 -42.917  < 2e-16 ***
## conflict_intensity           0.091108   0.002956  30.822  < 2e-16 ***
## territorial_losses_RSF      -0.071350   0.052376  -1.362 0.173115    
## territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 -0.139022   0.089299  -1.557 0.119515    
## log_population              -0.084356   0.024135  -3.495 0.000474 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for poisson family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 2755.9  on 194359  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 2408.5  on 194355  degrees of freedom
##   (2260 Beobachtungen als fehlend gelöscht)
## AIC: 2678.9
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 9
#deviance check, not good measure: deviance-based dispersion breaks down with extreme sparsity
deviance(pois_RSF) / df.residual(pois_RSF)
## [1] 0.0123921
#so pearson-based dispersion
sum(residuals(pois_RSF, type = "pearson")^2) / df.residual(pois_RSF)
## [1] 3.016552
#3.016

pois_SAF <- glm(
  hospital_attacks_SAF ~
    conflict_intensity +
    territorial_losses_SAF +
    log_population,
  data = panel_master_lag_actors,
  family = poisson()
)

summary(pois_RSF)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = hospital_attacks_RSF ~ conflict_intensity + territorial_losses_RSF + 
##     territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 + log_population, family = poisson(), 
##     data = panel_master_lag_actors)
## 
## Deviance Residuals: 
##     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  
## -2.1903  -0.0435  -0.0386  -0.0344  17.8450  
## 
## Coefficients:
##                              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                 -6.650530   0.154961 -42.917  < 2e-16 ***
## conflict_intensity           0.091108   0.002956  30.822  < 2e-16 ***
## territorial_losses_RSF      -0.071350   0.052376  -1.362 0.173115    
## territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 -0.139022   0.089299  -1.557 0.119515    
## log_population              -0.084356   0.024135  -3.495 0.000474 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for poisson family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 2755.9  on 194359  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 2408.5  on 194355  degrees of freedom
##   (2260 Beobachtungen als fehlend gelöscht)
## AIC: 2678.9
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 9
#check overdisperion
performance::check_overdispersion(pois_RSF)
## # Overdispersion test
## 
##        dispersion ratio =      3.017
##   Pearson's Chi-Squared = 586281.879
##                 p-value =    < 0.001
performance::check_overdispersion(pois_SAF)
## # Overdispersion test
## 
##        dispersion ratio =      1.530
##   Pearson's Chi-Squared = 300907.064
##                 p-value =    < 0.001
# Negative Binomial----
nb_RSF <- glm.nb(
  hospital_attacks_RSF ~
    conflict_intensity +
    territorial_losses_RSF +
    territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 +
    log_population,
  data = panel_master_lag_actors
)

summary(nb_RSF)
## 
## Call:
## glm.nb(formula = hospital_attacks_RSF ~ conflict_intensity + 
##     territorial_losses_RSF + territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 + log_population, 
##     data = panel_master_lag_actors, init.theta = 0.005347695394, 
##     link = log)
## 
## Deviance Residuals: 
##     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  
## -1.2030  -0.0326  -0.0289  -0.0257   4.4858  
## 
## Coefficients:
##                             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                 -7.16448    0.21145 -33.883  < 2e-16 ***
## conflict_intensity           1.36476    0.03421  39.889  < 2e-16 ***
## territorial_losses_RSF      -6.70778    0.57833 -11.599  < 2e-16 ***
## territorial_losses_RSF_lag1  1.33749    0.48253   2.772  0.00557 ** 
## log_population              -0.08882    0.03261  -2.724  0.00645 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for Negative Binomial(0.0053) family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 1008.75  on 194359  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  650.95  on 194355  degrees of freedom
##   (2260 Beobachtungen als fehlend gelöscht)
## AIC: 1959.4
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 1
## 
## 
##               Theta:  0.005348 
##           Std. Err.:  0.000871 
## Warning while fitting theta: Grenze der Alternierungen erreicht 
## 
##  2 x log-likelihood:  -1947.445000
nb_SAF <- glm.nb(
  hospital_attacks_SAF ~
    conflict_intensity +
    territorial_losses_SAF +
    log_population,
  data = panel_master_lag_actors
)

summary(nb_SAF)
## 
## Call:
## glm.nb(formula = hospital_attacks_SAF ~ conflict_intensity + 
##     territorial_losses_SAF + log_population, data = panel_master_lag_actors, 
##     init.theta = 0.0001910961067, link = log)
## 
## Deviance Residuals: 
##      Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max  
## -0.20527  -0.01562  -0.01338  -0.01119   2.06658  
## 
## Coefficients:
##                        Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)            -10.0800     0.6854 -14.707  < 2e-16 ***
## conflict_intensity       1.1958     0.1631   7.334 2.23e-13 ***
## territorial_losses_SAF  -5.2817     4.2694  -1.237   0.2160    
## log_population           0.1506     0.0851   1.770   0.0767 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for Negative Binomial(2e-04) family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 124.885  on 196619  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  81.959  on 196616  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 790.99
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 1
## 
## 
##               Theta:  0.0001911 
##           Std. Err.:  1.95e-05 
## Warning while fitting theta: Grenze der Alternierungen erreicht 
## 
##  2 x log-likelihood:  -780.9860000
#compare AIC (possion, negative binomial, ZINB)
AIC(pois_RSF, nb_RSF, zinb_RSF_t)
##            df      AIC
## pois_RSF    5 2678.945
## nb_RSF      6 1959.445
## zinb_RSF_t 11 1689.345
AIC(pois_SAF, nb_SAF, zinb_SAF_t)
##            df      AIC
## pois_SAF    4 933.7257
## nb_SAF      5 790.9856
## zinb_SAF_t  9 680.7296
#test overdispersion ----
lrtest(pois_RSF, nb_RSF)
## Likelihood ratio test
## 
## Model 1: hospital_attacks_RSF ~ conflict_intensity + territorial_losses_RSF + 
##     territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 + log_population
## Model 2: hospital_attacks_RSF ~ conflict_intensity + territorial_losses_RSF + 
##     territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 + log_population
##   #Df   LogLik Df Chisq Pr(>Chisq)    
## 1   5 -1334.47                        
## 2   6  -973.72  1 721.5  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
lrtest(pois_SAF, nb_SAF)
## Likelihood ratio test
## 
## Model 1: hospital_attacks_SAF ~ conflict_intensity + territorial_losses_SAF + 
##     log_population
## Model 2: hospital_attacks_SAF ~ conflict_intensity + territorial_losses_SAF + 
##     log_population
##   #Df  LogLik Df  Chisq Pr(>Chisq)    
## 1   4 -462.86                         
## 2   5 -390.49  1 144.74  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
#vuong test 
vuong(nb_RSF, zinb_RSF_t)
## Vuong Non-Nested Hypothesis Test-Statistic: 
## (test-statistic is asymptotically distributed N(0,1) under the
##  null that the models are indistinguishible)
## -------------------------------------------------------------
##               Vuong z-statistic             H_A    p-value
## Raw                   -5.997911 model2 > model1 9.9936e-10
## AIC-corrected         -5.783776 model2 > model1 3.6521e-09
## BIC-corrected         -4.694101 model2 > model1 1.3389e-06
vuong(nb_SAF, zinb_SAF_t)
## Vuong Non-Nested Hypothesis Test-Statistic: 
## (test-statistic is asymptotically distributed N(0,1) under the
##  null that the models are indistinguishible)
## -------------------------------------------------------------
##               Vuong z-statistic             H_A    p-value
## Raw                   -3.095053 model2 > model1 0.00098389
## AIC-corrected         -2.885673 model2 > model1 0.00195289
## BIC-corrected         -1.818985 model2 > model1 0.03445689
#check overdispersion ----
zinb_RSF_t$theta
## [1] 0.2372858
zinb_SAF_t$theta
## [1] 0.08817008
# - overdispersion is a thing - ZINB is valid!

#check residuals ----
plot_residuals_zinb_RSF <- plot(residuals(zinb_RSF_t, type = "pearson"))

print(plot_residuals_zinb_RSF)
## NULL
plot_resdiuals_zinb_SAF <- plot(residuals(zinb_SAF_t, type = "pearson"))

print(plot_resdiuals_zinb_SAF)
## NULL
#sparsity checks ----
#for territorial losses SAF lagged 
panel_master_lag_actors %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_nonzero = sum(territorial_losses_SAF_lag1 != 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_nonzero = mean(territorial_losses_SAF_lag1 != 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##        n n_nonzero share_nonzero
##    <int>     <int>         <dbl>
## 1 196620        73      0.000376
#variable ever switch on when attacks occur?
with(panel_master_lag_actors,
     table(territorial_losses_SAF_lag1 > 0,
           hospital_attacks_SAF > 0))
##        
##          FALSE   TRUE
##   FALSE 194249     38
##   TRUE      72      1
#territorial losses SAF 
panel_master_lag_actors %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_nonzero = sum(territorial_losses_SAF != 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_nonzero = mean(territorial_losses_SAF != 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##        n n_nonzero share_nonzero
##    <int>     <int>         <dbl>
## 1 196620        73      0.000371
#variable ever switch on when attacks occur?
with(panel_master_lag_actors,
     table(territorial_losses_SAF > 0,
           hospital_attacks_SAF > 0))
##        
##          FALSE   TRUE
##   FALSE 196509     38
##   TRUE      71      2
#territorial losses RSF lagged
panel_master_lag_actors %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_nonzero = sum(territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 != 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_nonzero = mean(territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 != 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##        n n_nonzero share_nonzero
##    <int>     <int>         <dbl>
## 1 196620        63      0.000324
#variable ever switch on when attacks occur?
with(panel_master_lag_actors,
     table(territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 > 0,
           hospital_attacks_RSF > 0))
##        
##          FALSE   TRUE
##   FALSE 194185    112
##   TRUE      57      6
#territorial losses RSF 
panel_master_lag_actors %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_nonzero = sum(territorial_losses_RSF != 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_nonzero = mean(territorial_losses_RSF != 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##        n n_nonzero share_nonzero
##    <int>     <int>         <dbl>
## 1 196620        63      0.000320
#variable ever switch on when attacks occur?
with(panel_master_lag_actors,
     table(territorial_losses_RSF > 0,
           hospital_attacks_RSF > 0))
##        
##          FALSE   TRUE
##   FALSE 196446    111
##   TRUE      56      7
#conflict Intensity 
panel_master_lag_actors %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_nonzero = sum(conflict_intensity != 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_nonzero = mean(conflict_intensity != 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##        n n_nonzero share_nonzero
##    <int>     <int>         <dbl>
## 1 196620      1761       0.00896
#variable ever switch on when attacks occur?
with(panel_master_lag_actors,
     table(conflict_intensity > 0,
           hospital_attacks_RSF > 0))
##        
##          FALSE   TRUE
##   FALSE 194802     57
##   TRUE    1700     61
#variable ever switch on when attacks occur?
with(panel_master_lag_actors,
     table(conflict_intensity > 0,
           hospital_attacks_SAF > 0))
##        
##          FALSE   TRUE
##   FALSE 194837     22
##   TRUE    1743     18
#conflict intensity lagged
panel_master_lag_actors %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_nonzero = sum(conflict_intensity_lag1 != 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_nonzero = mean(conflict_intensity_lag1 != 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##        n n_nonzero share_nonzero
##    <int>     <int>         <dbl>
## 1 196620      1761       0.00906
#variable ever switch on when attacks occur?
with(panel_master_lag_actors,
     table(conflict_intensity_lag1 > 0,
           hospital_attacks_RSF > 0))
##        
##          FALSE   TRUE
##   FALSE 192538     61
##   TRUE    1704     57
#variable ever switch on when attacks occur?
with(panel_master_lag_actors,
     table(conflict_intensity_lag1 > 0,
           hospital_attacks_SAF > 0))
##        
##          FALSE   TRUE
##   FALSE 192574     25
##   TRUE    1747     14
#separation check ----
with(panel_master_lag_actors,
     table(territorial_losses_SAF_lag1 > 0,
           hospital_attacks_SAF == 0))
##        
##          FALSE   TRUE
##   FALSE     38 194249
##   TRUE       1     72
#alternative check to sparsity check----
panel_master_lag_actors %>%
  filter(hospital_attacks_SAF > 0) %>%
  summarise(
    n_attacks = n(),
    share_loss_now = mean(territorial_losses_SAF != 0),
    share_loss_lag = mean(territorial_losses_SAF_lag1 != 0)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   n_attacks share_loss_now share_loss_lag
##       <int>          <dbl>          <dbl>
## 1        40           0.05             NA
#contingency table
with(panel_master_lag_actors,
     table(
       loss = territorial_losses_SAF_lag1 > 0,
       attack = hospital_attacks_SAF > 0
     ))
##        attack
## loss     FALSE   TRUE
##   FALSE 194249     38
##   TRUE      72      1
panel_master_lag_actors %>%
  filter(hospital_attacks_RSF > 0) %>%
  summarise(
    n_attacks = n(),
    share_loss_now = mean(territorial_losses_RSF != 0),
    share_loss_lag = mean(territorial_losses_RSF_lag1 != 0)
  )
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   n_attacks share_loss_now share_loss_lag
##       <int>          <dbl>          <dbl>
## 1       118         0.0593         0.0508
